prompt_text:
Analyze the following java classes for violations of the Open/Closed Principle (OCP).

For each class you are given:
- Its full source body
- Its base class bodies (where available in the repo)
- The file's imports
- Short headers of sibling classes in the same file

Use the inheritance and import context to reason about dependencies and substitutability.

## Principle Definition
Software entities should be open for extension but closed for modification. When adding new behavior requires modifyi...

  "findings": [
    {
      "file_path": "gson/gson/src/main/java/com/google/gson/GsonBuilder.java",
      "entity_name": "GsonBuilder.createFactories",
      "entity_type": "method",
      "line_start": 700,
      "line_end": 766,
      "principle": "OCP",
      "severity": "high",
      "description": "Hardcoded list of built-in TypeAdapterFactories violates OCP.",
      "reasoning": "The `createFactories` method explicitly adds a fixed list of `TypeAdapterFactory` instances for various types. If Gson needs to support a new built-in type or a new way of handling an existing type, this method must be modified to add the new factory. This makes the class closed for extension without modification."
    },
    {
      "file_path": "gson/gson/src/main/java/com/google/gson/GsonBuilder.java",
      "entity_name": "GsonBuilder.addDateTypeAdapters",

      "line_start": 790,
      "line_end": 809,
      "principle": "OCP",
      "severity": "medium",
      "description": "Conditional logic for date type adapters based on configuration violates OCP.",
      "reasoning": "The `addDateTypeAdapters` method uses `if-else if` statements to determine which date format (pattern-based or style-based) to use and whether to add SQL date type adapters. Adding a new date format or a new SQL date type would require modifying this method, making it not open for extension."
    },
    {
      "file_path": "gson/gson/src/main/java/com/google/gson/stream/JsonWriter.java",
      "entity_name": "JsonWriter.value(Number)",
      "entity_type": "method",
      "line_start": 400,
      "line_end": 425,
      "principle": "OCP",
      "severity": "medium",
      "description": "Conditional logic and hardcoded checks for specific Number subclasses in `value(Number)` and `alwaysCreatesValidJsonNumber` methods violate OCP.",
      "reasoning": "The `value(Number)` method and its helper `alwaysCreatesValidJsonNumber` contain `if-else if` chains and direct class comparisons (`c == Integer.class || c == Long.class || ...`) to determine if a `Number` subclass always produces a valid JSON number string. If a new `Number` subclass were introduced that also always produces a valid JSON number, these methods would need to be modified, violating OCP."
    },

findings.description: Hardcoded list of built-in TypeAdapterFactories violates OCP.
findings.reasoning: The `createFactories` method explicitly adds a fixed list of `TypeAdapterFactory` instances for various types. If Gson needs to support a new built-in type or a new way of handling an existing type, this method must be modified to add the new factory. This makes the class closed for extension without modification.

findings.description: Conditional logic for date type adapters based on configuration violates OCP.
findings.reasoning: The `addDateTypeAdapters` method uses `if-else if` statements to determine which date format (pattern-based or style-based) to use and whether to add SQL date type adapters. Adding a new date format or a new SQL date type would require modifying this method, making it not open for extension.

findings.description: Conditional logic and hardcoded checks for specific Number subclasses in `value(Number)` and `alwaysCreatesValidJsonNumber` methods violate OCP.
findings.reasoning: The `value(Number)` method and its helper `alwaysCreatesValidJsonNumber` contain `if-else if` chains and direct class comparisons (`c == Integer.class || c == Long.class || ...`) to determine if a `Number` subclass always produces a valid JSON number string. If a new `Number` subclass were introduced that also always produces a valid JSON number, these methods would need to be modified, violating OCP.
